title: Gratiola amphiantha
intro: Gratiola amphiantha, also known as the fairy bellflower, is a rare and unique herbaceous perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, Gratiola amphiantha has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help soothe coughs and ease congestion. The bioactive constituents of Gratiola amphiantha include iridoid glycosides, such as gratiolin, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antioxidant activities. In herbal medicine, Gratiola amphiantha is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues and to promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Gratiola amphiantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Gratiola and is often classified as Gratiola amphiantha (Small) Pennell. The classification of Gratiola amphiantha is further supported by its unique characteristics and shared traits with other members of the Plantaginaceae family.
